meta.title: Pearl River Delta
meta.subtitle: Industry-cluster overview
meta.tagline: The world's factory floor is not one place — it's a delta of towns, each making one thing better than anywhere else. This is the cluster map across the nine PRD cities.
meta.methodology: Town and district coordinates are from Google Places, converted from China's GCJ-02 datum to WGS-84 to sit correctly on the OpenStreetMap / CARTO basemap. Each pin marks a town's dominant manufacturing or trade specialty drawn from current sourcing guides; clusters are indicative, not exclusive, and shift over time. Scope is the nine-city Pearl River Delta; Hong Kong, Macau and Chaoshan are outside it.

clusters.note: China's Lighting Capital — roughly 70% of national lighting output; thousands of LED and fixture factories and showrooms.
Part of the Guzhen lighting belt; strong in outdoor and decorative lighting.
Locks, hardware and electronic acoustics; a century-old metalworking base.
Small household electrical appliances cluster.
Mahogany and rosewood furniture.
Fashion and women's garments; one of China's biggest clothing manufacturing and wholesale towns.
China's sweater capital — the largest source of wool knitwear, with a major wool wholesale market.
Precision molds, metalwork and CNC; deep smartphone supply chain (OPPO/vivo nearby).
One of Dongguan's largest electronics and electrical-equipment clusters.
Furniture and furniture machinery, shoe materials, and major trade fairs.
China's furniture trade capital — Louvre, Sunlink and Tuanyi markets stretch for kilometres.
Furniture manufacturing base feeding the Lecong markets.
Home-appliance heartland; headquarters of Midea.
China's largest ceramics and tile center; hundreds of tile producers and expo markets.
Aluminium profiles and non-ferrous metals.
Textiles and fabrics — a major woven and knit fabric base.
Lingerie and underwear cluster.
The fabric Huaqiangbei — ~18 buildings and dozens of markets trading 100,000+ fabrics, plus Guangzhou's wider wholesale market belt.
A major gold and fine-jewelry manufacturing base supplying brands worldwide.
China's leather-goods and handbag capital, in Huadu.
Automobile manufacturing (Toyota JV) and a deep-water port.
The world's electronics market and the trading face of the delta's electronics supply chain. See the building-level deep-dive.
PCBA and electronics contract manufacturing across Bao'an's industrial belt.
Tech and R&D core — drones (DJI), comms and hardware design houses.
Gold and jewelry manufacturing and wholesale hub; hundreds of gold counters in Luohu.
EV and battery manufacturing; BYD's base.
World hub for printer ink and toner cartridges and remanufactured consumables; also air conditioning (Gree).
China's faucet and bathroom-hardware town, in Kaiping.
One of China's biggest jade carving and trading centers.
Electronics, batteries and display — TCL, Desay and EVE Energy.

orientation.v: Shenzhen, Dongguan, Huizhou — electronics, IT, EV/batteries. Shenzhen designs and sells; Dongguan and Huizhou build.
Zhuhai, Zhongshan, Jiangmen — light manufacturing: lighting, hardware, appliances, consumables.
Guangzhou, Foshan, Zhaoqing — wholesale trade, furniture, ceramics, jewelry, textiles.
Intercity rail and metro tie the delta together; Shenzhen, Dongguan and Guangzhou are reachable in a day.
The market deep-dives (Huaqiangbei, Yiwu) are where goods are traded; this map is where they are made.

notes: “One town, one product”: each cluster concentrates a full supply chain — materials, tooling, assembly and logistics — within a few kilometres, which is why lead times and minimums there beat sourcing the same part cold.
A cluster marks the dominant local industry, not the only one. Big districts (Nanshan, Panyu, Bao'an) make far more than one thing.
Trading hubs and making hubs differ: Guangzhou and Shenzhen design, brand and wholesale; Dongguan, Foshan and Zhongshan run the factories.
Just beyond the delta: Chaoshan in eastern Guangdong (Chenghai toys, Chaozhou ceramics and sanitaryware) is a separate cluster world about three hours east.
